name: ctpV
function: P-type ATPase involved in zinc efflux, likely contributing to resistance against zinc poisoning induced by host macrophages.  The ctpC gene encodes a putative P1B-type ATPase that exports zinc from mycobacteria under oxidative conditions, protecting the bacteria from zinc toxicity. It is also involved in intracellular replication of *M. tuberculosis*.
drug_susc_impact: Reduces *Mtb*'s drug susceptibility by enhancing its ability to tolerate zinc toxicity within the macrophage phagosome. The gene is highly sensitive to zinc, suggesting it plays a role in drug susceptibility. Increased zinc concentration induces expression of the gene.
infection_impact: Contributes to *Mtb*'s intracellular survival and replication within macrophages by facilitating zinc efflux. The gene is essential for intracellular replication of *M. tuberculosis*.
